title: PDFExtractorOutput
description: Structured output for a single donor agreement document

processed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the document was processed

clauses.description: All classified clauses extracted from the document

text.description: Full sentence or clause text as it appears in the document
text.example: The grantee must submit quarterly financial reports.

page.description: 1-based page number where the clause was found

trigger_word.description: The modal or context verb that triggered classification

tier.description: Classification tier. RESTRICTION/QUALIFIED_RESTRICTION/GUIDED_DISCRETION/DECISION are the NGO obligation ladder. DONOR_RIGHT is a separate axis for donor power clauses.

context_flag.description: True if the clause was flagged via a context_pattern verb-first phrase

actor.description: Who bears the obligation or exercises the power: NGO (default) or DONOR

decision_type.description: Sub-type of DECISION clauses only. DISCRETIONARY_AUTONOMY: real NGO choice, no approval needed. CONDITIONAL_FLEXIBILITY: NGO nominally but requires prior donor approval. Null for all non-DECISION tiers.

donor_right_type.description: Sub-type of DONOR_RIGHT clauses, or embedded donor right in mixed clauses (see contains_donor_right). AUDIT_RIGHT: inspection/investigation. SUSPENSION_RIGHT: halt disbursements. TERMINATION_RIGHT: revoke/terminate grant. INTERVENTION_RIGHT: intervene in operations. Null for pure NGO obligation clauses with no embedded donor right.

contains_donor_right.description: True when the clause is primarily an NGO obligation but also contains embedded donor power language. When true, donor_right_type identifies which type of donor right is embedded. Always false for pure DONOR_RIGHT clauses (the tier itself conveys this) and pure NGO obligation clauses.

preference_signal.description: For GUIDED_DISCRETION clauses: the substance of what the donor prefers or recommends. Null for all other tiers.

preference_strength.description: For GUIDED_DISCRETION clauses: strong (should/ideally — audit-adjacent, donor expects this) or soft (recommended/encouraged/advisable — genuine preference, no enforcement risk). Null for all other tiers.

creates_ngo_dependency.description: True when the NGO cannot act or proceed until the donor completes an action. Applies to DONOR_RIGHT and mixed clauses where the donor action blocks NGO progress.

dead_end.description: True when a RESTRICTION or QUALIFIED_RESTRICTION clause matches a dead_end signal phrase. Always false for GUIDED_DISCRETION, DECISION, and DONOR_RIGHT.

dead_end_type.description: Type of dead end. UNCONDITIONAL: no conditional language in the clause — pooling candidate (requires human verification). CONDITIONAL: conditional language present (if/when/provided that) — permanent pooling exclusion. AMBIGUOUS: genuine unresolved legal carve-out in the clause text — excluded until resolved. Null when dead_end is false.

domain.description: Primary semantic domain of the clause, used for cross-donor pooling

notes.description: Optional analyst notes; includes matched qualifier phrase for QUALIFIED_RESTRICTION clauses and ambiguous phrase for AMBIGUOUS dead ends
